title: Exception Handling Practice
instructions: Practice using try-except blocks to handle errors gracefully. Implement safe operations with proper error handling.
hints: Use try-except with ValueError and TypeError
Catch KeyError and TypeError for dict access
Catch IndexError and TypeError for list access

testCases.description: safe_int should convert valid string numbers to integers
safe_int should return default value for non-numeric strings
safe_divide should return None when dividing by zero
testCases.validation: assert safe_divide(10, 0) is None, 'safe_divide(10, 0) should return None'

testCases.description: safe_divide should handle valid division correctly
get_dict_value should return default when key is missing
testCases.validation: assert get_dict_value({'a': 1}, 'b', 'N/A') == 'N/A', 'get_dict_value should return default for missing key'

testCases.description: safe_list_get should return default when index is out of range
testCases.validation: assert safe_list_get([1, 2, 3], 10, 'oops') == 'oops', 'safe_list_get with bad index should return default'

description: Practice Python exception handling with try-except blocks. Implement safe type conversion that handles ValueError and TypeError, division that catches ZeroDivisionError, and safe access patterns for dictionaries (KeyError) and lists (IndexError). Graceful error handling is essential for building robust, production-ready Python applications.
